Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{"labels":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository."},"item_number":{"type":"number","description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}}]}}

2026-03-20T02:55:56.0466520Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:19.649203253Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57275,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-20T02:55:56.0490308Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:19.894641145Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57275,\"title\":\"SDK Validation - .NET should only be required for the new mgmt csharp emitter, not for swagger-based generation\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIn TypeSpec migration PRs (e.g., [Azure/azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000)), `SDK Validation - .NET` fails during swagger-based SDK generation and causes `SDK Validation Status` to be marked as **required and failed**, blocking the PR from merging.\\n\\n### Specific Example\\n\\nIn [azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000) (TypeSpec conversion for reservations):\\n- The PR adds TypeSpec files for the reservations service\\n- The `tspconfig.yaml` has **no C# emitter configuration** — only Python, Java, JS, and Go emitters are configured\\n- The .NET SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.Reservations`) is still swagger-based (uses `autorest.md`, no `tsp-location.yaml`)\\n- SDK Validation - .NET runs and generates from swagger, which **fails**\\n- Because the PR has TypeSpec files, `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` is set to `true` for .NET management plane\\n- This causes `SDK Validation Status` to fail and block the PR\\n\\nSee comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000#issuecomment-4073647992\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination in the spec repo\\u0026#39;s `spec-gen-sdk-runner` ([`getRequiredSettingValue()`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/main/eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts)) is **too broad** for .NET:\\n\\n```typescript\\n// eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts\\nexport function getRequiredSettingValue(\\n  hasManagementPlaneSpecs: boolean,\\n  hasTypeSpecProjects: boolean,\\n  sdkName: SdkName,\\n): boolean {\\n  // If the SDK is azure-sdk-for-net, return false if there are no TypeSpec projects.\\n  if (sdkName === \\u0026#34;azure-sdk-for-net\\u0026#34; \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !hasTypeSpecProjects) {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  if (hasManagementPlaneSpecs) {\\n    return SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[sdkName].managementPlane; // true for .NET\\n  }\\n  ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor .NET, the check is required when `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` AND the spec is management plane. But `hasTypeSpecProjects` is determined by whether the PR has **any** TypeSpec files — it does **not** check if the TypeSpec project has a C# emitter configured (`@azure-tools/typespec-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, or `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor the reservations PR:\\n- `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` (PR has TypeSpec files)\\n- `hasManagementPlaneSpecs = true` (management plane service)\\n- `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[\\u0026#34;azure-sdk-for-net\\u0026#34;].managementPlane = true`\\n- Result: `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired = true` — **incorrect** because the .NET SDK has no TypeSpec generation path\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\n`SDK Validation - .NET` should only be **required** when the TypeSpec project uses the **new mgmt csharp emitter** (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor management plane services NOT using the new mgmt emitter (still swagger-based or using older emitters), SDK Validation - .NET should be **optional** (not blocking).\\n\\n## Proposed Fix\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination should be moved to the SDK repo\\u0026#39;s generation script, which already has the logic to detect the new mgmt emitter.\\n\\nIn [`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1), the function `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` (lines 48-70) already correctly detects whether a package uses the new mgmt csharp emitter:\\n\\n```powershell\\nfunction Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator {\\n    param(\\n        [string]$serviceType,\\n        [string]$tspConfigFile,\\n        [string]$sdkProjectFolder\\n    )\\n    if ($serviceType -ne \\u0026#39;resource-manager\\u0026#39;) { return $false }\\n    # checks tspconfig.yaml for @azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n    # checks tsp-location.yaml for mgmt emitter package json path\\n    ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Suggested approach:\\n1. **In the SDK repo** (`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`): Output a property like `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` in the generation output JSON, set to `true` only when the package uses the new mgmt emitter (per `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator`), or for data-plane TypeSpec packages.\\n2. **In the spec repo** (`spec-gen-sdk-runner`): Read the SDK repo\\u0026#39;s `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` value from the generation output/execution report, instead of computing it from the broad `hasTypeSpecProjects` flag.\\n\\n## Relevant Files\\n\\n| File | Repo | Role |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| `eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1` | azure-sdk-for-net | SDK generation script with `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `getRequiredSettingValue()` and `generateArtifact()` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/types.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings` definition |\\n| `.github/workflows/src/spec-gen-sdk-status.js` | azure-rest-api-specs | Reads `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` from artifact |\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57275\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T02:52:47Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-20T02:52:47Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-20T02:55:56.0526615Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:24.111620775Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"missing required parameter: method","type":"text"}],"isError":true}}}
2026-03-20T02:55:56.0528222Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:24.120772841Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57275,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-20T02:55:56.0551491Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:24.329599192Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57275,\"title\":\"SDK Validation - .NET should only be required for the new mgmt csharp emitter, not for swagger-based generation\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIn TypeSpec migration PRs (e.g., [Azure/azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000)), `SDK Validation - .NET` fails during swagger-based SDK generation and causes `SDK Validation Status` to be marked as **required and failed**, blocking the PR from merging.\\n\\n### Specific Example\\n\\nIn [azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000) (TypeSpec conversion for reservations):\\n- The PR adds TypeSpec files for the reservations service\\n- The `tspconfig.yaml` has **no C# emitter configuration** — only Python, Java, JS, and Go emitters are configured\\n- The .NET SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.Reservations`) is still swagger-based (uses `autorest.md`, no `tsp-location.yaml`)\\n- SDK Validation - .NET runs and generates from swagger, which **fails**\\n- Because the PR has TypeSpec files, `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` is set to `true` for .NET management plane\\n- This causes `SDK Validation Status` to fail and block the PR\\n\\nSee comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000#issuecomment-4073647992\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination in the spec repo\\u0026#39;s `spec-gen-sdk-runner` ([`getRequiredSettingValue()`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/main/eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts)) is **too broad** for .NET:\\n\\n```typescript\\n// eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts\\nexport function getRequiredSettingValue(\\n  hasManagementPlaneSpecs: boolean,\\n  hasTypeSpecProjects: boolean,\\n  sdkName: SdkName,\\n): boolean {\\n  // If the SDK is azure-sdk-for-net, return false if there are no TypeSpec projects.\\n  if (sdkName === \\u0026#34;azure-sdk-for-net\\u0026#34; \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !hasTypeSpecProjects) {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  if (hasManagementPlaneSpecs) {\\n    return SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[sdkName].managementPlane; // true for .NET\\n  }\\n  ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor .NET, the check is required when `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` AND the spec is management plane. But `hasTypeSpecProjects` is determined by whether the PR has **any** TypeSpec files — it does **not** check if the TypeSpec project has a C# emitter configured (`@azure-tools/typespec-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, or `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor the reservations PR:\\n- `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` (PR has TypeSpec files)\\n- `hasManagementPlaneSpecs = true` (management plane service)\\n- `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[\\u0026#34;azure-sdk-for-net\\u0026#34;].managementPlane = true`\\n- Result: `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired = true` — **incorrect** because the .NET SDK has no TypeSpec generation path\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\n`SDK Validation - .NET` should only be **required** when the TypeSpec project uses the **new mgmt csharp emitter** (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor management plane services NOT using the new mgmt emitter (still swagger-based or using older emitters), SDK Validation - .NET should be **optional** (not blocking).\\n\\n## Proposed Fix\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination should be moved to the SDK repo\\u0026#39;s generation script, which already has the logic to detect the new mgmt emitter.\\n\\nIn [`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1), the function `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` (lines 48-70) already correctly detects whether a package uses the new mgmt csharp emitter:\\n\\n```powershell\\nfunction Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator {\\n    param(\\n        [string]$serviceType,\\n        [string]$tspConfigFile,\\n        [string]$sdkProjectFolder\\n    )\\n    if ($serviceType -ne \\u0026#39;resource-manager\\u0026#39;) { return $false }\\n    # checks tspconfig.yaml for @azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n    # checks tsp-location.yaml for mgmt emitter package json path\\n    ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Suggested approach:\\n1. **In the SDK repo** (`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`): Output a property like `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` in the generation output JSON, set to `true` only when the package uses the new mgmt emitter (per `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator`), or for data-plane TypeSpec packages.\\n2. **In the spec repo** (`spec-gen-sdk-runner`): Read the SDK repo\\u0026#39;s `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` value from the generation output/execution report, instead of computing it from the broad `hasTypeSpecProjects` flag.\\n\\n## Relevant Files\\n\\n| File | Repo | Role |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| `eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1` | azure-sdk-for-net | SDK generation script with `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `getRequiredSettingValue()` and `generateArtifact()` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/types.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings` definition |\\n| `.github/workflows/src/spec-gen-sdk-status.js` | azure-rest-api-specs | Reads `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` from artifact |\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57275\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T02:52:47Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-20T02:52:47Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-20T02:55:56.0609806Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T02:55:28.46698843Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4105389522,\"number\":57275,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"SDK Validation - .NET should only be required for the new mgmt csharp emitter, not for swagger-based generation\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIn TypeSpec migration PRs (e.g., [Azure/azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000)), `SDK Validation - .NET` fails during swagger-based SDK generation and causes `SDK Validation Status` to be marked as **required and failed**, blocking the PR from merging.\\n\\n### Specific Example\\n\\nIn [azure-rest-api-specs#41000](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000) (TypeSpec conversion for reservations):\\n- The PR adds TypeSpec files for the reservations service\\n- The `tspconfig.yaml` has **no C# emitter configuration** — only Python, Java, JS, and Go emitters are configured\\n- The .NET SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.Reservations`) is still swagger-based (uses `autorest.md`, no `tsp-location.yaml`)\\n- SDK Validation - .NET runs and generates from swagger, which **fails**\\n- Because the PR has TypeSpec files, `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` is set to `true` for .NET management plane\\n- This causes `SDK Validation Status` to fail and block the PR\\n\\nSee comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41000#issuecomment-4073647992\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination in the spec repo's `spec-gen-sdk-runner` ([`getRequiredSettingValue()`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/main/eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts)) is **too broad** for .NET:\\n\\n```typescript\\n// eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts\\nexport function getRequiredSettingValue(\\n  hasManagementPlaneSpecs: boolean,\\n  hasTypeSpecProjects: boolean,\\n  sdkName: SdkName,\\n): boolean {\\n  // If the SDK is azure-sdk-for-net, return false if there are no TypeSpec projects.\\n  if (sdkName === \\\"azure-sdk-for-net\\\" \\u0026\\u0026 !hasTypeSpecProjects) {\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  if (hasManagementPlaneSpecs) {\\n    return SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[sdkName].managementPlane; // true for .NET\\n  }\\n  ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor .NET, the check is required when `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` AND the spec is management plane. But `hasTypeSpecProjects` is determined by whether the PR has **any** TypeSpec files — it does **not** check if the TypeSpec project has a C# emitter configured (`@azure-tools/typespec-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, or `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor the reservations PR:\\n- `hasTypeSpecProjects = true` (PR has TypeSpec files)\\n- `hasManagementPlaneSpecs = true` (management plane service)\\n- `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings[\\\"azure-sdk-for-net\\\"].managementPlane = true`\\n- Result: `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired = true` — **incorrect** because the .NET SDK has no TypeSpec generation path\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\n`SDK Validation - .NET` should only be **required** when the TypeSpec project uses the **new mgmt csharp emitter** (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).\\n\\nFor management plane services NOT using the new mgmt emitter (still swagger-based or using older emitters), SDK Validation - .NET should be **optional** (not blocking).\\n\\n## Proposed Fix\\n\\nThe `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` determination should be moved to the SDK repo's generation script, which already has the logic to detect the new mgmt emitter.\\n\\nIn [`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1), the function `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` (lines 48-70) already correctly detects whether a package uses the new mgmt csharp emitter:\\n\\n```powershell\\nfunction Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator {\\n    param(\\n        [string]$serviceType,\\n        [string]$tspConfigFile,\\n        [string]$sdkProjectFolder\\n    )\\n    if ($serviceType -ne 'resource-manager') { return $false }\\n    # checks tspconfig.yaml for @azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n    # checks tsp-location.yaml for mgmt emitter package json path\\n    ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Suggested approach:\\n1. **In the SDK repo** (`Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1`): Output a property like `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` in the generation output JSON, set to `true` only when the package uses the new mgmt emitter (per `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator`), or for data-plane TypeSpec packages.\\n2. **In the spec repo** (`spec-gen-sdk-runner`): Read the SDK repo's `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` value from the generation output/execution report, instead of computing it from the broad `hasTypeSpecProjects` flag.\\n\\n## Relevant Files\\n\\n| File | Repo | Role |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| `eng/scripts/Invoke-GenerateAndBuildV2.ps1` | azure-sdk-for-net | SDK generation script with `Test-MgmtSdkUsingNewGenerator` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/command-helpers.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `getRequiredSettingValue()` and `generateArtifact()` |\\n| `eng/tools/spec-gen-sdk-runner/src/types.ts` | azure-rest-api-specs | `SpecGenSdkRequiredSettings` definition |\\n| `.github/workflows/src/spec-gen-sdk-status.js` | azure-rest-api-specs | Reads `isSpecGenSdkCheckRequired` from artifact
